summary: Route panel: prove the uniform local-1-D incidence inequality four independent ways, judge, and adversarially stress-test the strongest route

routes.route: ROUTE 1 — arithmetic / kappa_3 bound on the uniform local-1-D incidence c_k(q,rho) <= C*rho+1.
routes.proof_sketch:
Setup. Build the level-k walk as chain = anchors[0] then per-segment word_interiors(anchors[i],words[i])+anchors[i+1] from gate2-l7-construction-L{5,6,7}.pkl (8214 / 27697 / 92732 distinct lattice points, all a triple-free path).

Route-1 argument attempted. Fix a Chebyshev ball B(q,rho) containing N walk points, rho in [1,10]. Take any in-ball walk point p0 as anchor. Triple-freeness => the N-1 chords p0->p_i have pairwise-distinct primitive directions (two parallel chords would give 3 collinear walk points). I VERIFIED this holds exactly: in every densest ball, #distinct primitive chord directions == N-1. So N-1 <= (number of undirected primitive directions realizable inside the ball).

Arithmetic input (kappa_3). The proven fact is max kappa_3 = 12, where kappa_3(a,b)=v_3(a x b) (3-adic valuation of the cross product / projective 3-adic collision depth). For Route 1 to cap N it would have to force chords to collide 3-adically. It does the OPPOSITE of helping locally: for two chords of sup-norm <= 2rho (rho<=10, so sup-norm <= 20) every component of a x b is a_i b_j - a_j b_i with |.| <= 8 rho^2 <= 800. A nonzero integer <= 800 has v_3 <= floor(log_3 800) = 6, UNCONDITIONALLY. Reaching kappa_3 = 12 needs |a x b| >= 3^12 = 531441, i.e. chords of Chebyshev length ~515 — two orders of magnitude outside any rho<=10 ball. Hence the proven bound kappa_3 <= 12 is VACUOUS at local scales and contributes nothing.

What survives is only the triple-free direction count: N-1 <= geomcap(2rho) = #primitive dirs of sup-norm <= 2rho, which I measured grows like rho^{2.9} (24,144,432,1008,1872,5040,14256 for rho=1..10). At the load-bearing rho=4.44 this caps N at ~1009 — vs the target 12, loose by ~84x. So Route 1 yields a level-independent but hopelessly non-linear (O(rho^3)) bound; it cannot deliver O(rho).

Positive control: the target inequality itself is TRUE on the data (exact max over all integer lattice centres at rho=4 is 10/10/12 for L5/L6/L7; walk-centred rho=10 maxima 25/26/27; implied C level-stable ~2.5). It just is not the kappa_3 mechanism that makes it true — the O(rho) curve-like law is metric (M's singular values 2.54/3.00/3.54 all >1 give directional contraction, plus the walk is a bounded-step path), not an arithmetic-multiplicity phenomenon.
routes.key_sublemma: "Points that are mutually incidence-rich (share high kappa_3) inside a small ball are limited by kappa_3=12, capping N." This sub-lemma is FALSE-as-useful: inside a rho<=10 ball no two chords can even reach kappa_3 > 6 (magnitude bound), and the walk realises only <=4, so the value 12 never activates — the premise "incidence-rich pairs exist locally" is empty.
routes.C_derived: Route 1 gives N <= geomcap(2rho)+1 ~ rho^2.9; at rho=4.44 that is ~1009, an effective C ~ 227 (per unit rho) — NOT the target. It does not yield any linear C. (The true empirical C, from other structure, is ~2.5–2.75: exact lattice max 12 at rho=4 on L7 => C=(12-1)/4.44=2.48.)
routes.computational_evidence: On real L5/L6/L7 walks: (1) triple-free exact — distinct chord directions == N-1 in every densest ball. (2) The kappa_3-based bound does NOT dominate the observed c_k; it exceeds it by ~2 orders of magnitude (bound 1009 vs observed 10–12 at rho~4). (3) Decisive: sampled 4032 anchors on L7, all chords to walk points within rho=10 — observed LOCAL max kappa_3 = 4, magnitude ceiling = 6, max |cross component| = 190 << 3^12 = 531441; local kappa_3 distribution {0:205513, 1:16029, 2:1617, 3:86, 4:1}. The proven kappa_3<=12 never binds locally. (4) Target inequality verified true anyway: exact all-lattice-centre max c_k at rho=4 = {L5:10, L6:10, L7:12} <= 12.
routes.script: All under /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route1/ (run with PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 and the pypy3 binary): measure_ck.py (walk-centred c_k, rho1-10), exact_lattice_max.py & exact_small.py & one_rho.py (exact max over ALL integer lattice centres via box-coverage), structure_probe.py (densest-ball dissection: distinct dirs, geomcap, local kappa_3, path-arc runs), local_kappa.py (the crux: local kappa_3 distribution vs magnitude ceiling on L7), geomcap.py (geometric-capacity growth exponent). Consolidated numbers in /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route1/RESULTS.json.
routes.where_stuck: The proven arithmetic fact (kappa_3 <= 12) is a LONG-RANGE statement: kappa_3=12 requires cross-product magnitude >= 3^12, achievable only by chords of Chebyshev length ~515. Inside a ball of radius rho <= 10 the cross products are bounded by 8*rho^2 <= 800, forcing kappa_3 <= 6 by magnitude alone (walk realises <=4). So kappa_3 is vacuous at exactly the scales the lemma needs, and the arithmetic route degenerates to the pure triple-free direction count, which is O(rho^3) — not the required O(rho). To get C*rho+1 one must import the METRIC/contraction structure (M singular values all >1 => the walk does not fold, so the ~geomcap available directions are NOT all realized; only ~2.5*rho are). That linear law lives in Route(s) using M-contraction + bounded-step path adjacency, not in kappa_3. Route 1 cannot be repaired to close the gap on its own.

routes.route: Route 2 — irrational rotation / equidistribution of local tangent directions under iterated dilation M (rotation angle arccos(-1/6)).
routes.proof_sketch:
GOAL: level-independent C with c_k(q,rho) <= C*rho + 1 for rho in [1,10].

Route-2 THESIS (as posed): M's rotation arccos(-1/6) is irrational, so tangent directions rotate irrationally and equidistribute across dilations; the walk therefore cannot keep re-entering a ball along aligned directions, so it stays curve-like (dim 1) and incidence is linear in rho.

WHAT I FORMALIZED AND TESTED. I rebuilt the real L5/L6/L7 chains (chain = anchors[0] then per-i word_interiors(anchors[i],words[i]) + anchors[i+1]); all three are exact paths (0 non-menu steps, all points distinct). For the densest Cheb-rho balls I decomposed the walk-in-ball into maximal contiguous ARCS and measured their tangent (chord) directions.

RESULT — the equidistribution premise FAILS at the per-ball scale, in two independent ways:
(1) The densest balls contain very FEW arcs — narcs = 1,2,3 even at rho=10 (e.g. L7 rho=10 densest ball: cnt=27, 3 arcs of length 14/6/7). The walk barely re-enters; there is almost nothing for direction-equidistribution to spread. The count is dominated by ARC LENGTH (~14 at rho=10), not re-entry multiplicity.
(2) When two arcs DO share a ball they are often NEARLY PARALLEL: worst |cos(arc_i,arc_j)| = 0.99 (L6 rho=10), 0.999 (L7 rho=4). So the walk DOES re-enter along aligned directions — the exact thing Route 2 claims cannot happen. Directions are NOT equidistributed in a ball; they are coherent.

WHY: the walk is BALLISTIC, not diffusive. Displacement over n steps scales as <|disp|> ~ n (2.93,5.28,9.78,18.36,...,225.9 for n=1..128, i.e. ~1.77 n), while MSD/n GROWS (8.76 -> 405) — ruling out diffusion (which would give <|disp|> ~ sqrt(n) and the local-equidistribution picture). Consecutive step directions are persistent: mean cos = 0.649 (lag 1), 0.498 (5), 0.394 (20), 0.321 (50). Local coherence, decaying slowly — a curve, not a random walk. Ballistic coherence is PRECISELY what makes incidence linear in rho (an arc crosses a rho-ball in O(rho) steps, giving O(rho) points; re-entries are O(1)). Local equidistribution would have given diffusive rho^2 incidence, contradicting the measured linear law.

WHAT ACTUALLY CLOSES THE INEQUALITY (found while executing Route 2): the metric-contraction COUNT-RECURSION, using only already-proven pieces:
 - Exact identity: Walk_{k+1} = (M.Walk_k) DISJOINT-UNION Stitch_k. VERIFIED: all 27,697 points of M.L6 lie in L7 (0 missing), so L7 = 27,697 dilated + 65,035 refill.
 - ||M^{-1}||_inf = 4/9 (recomputed exactly: M^{-1} rows have abs-sums 1/3, 4/9, 1/3). Hence M^{-1} B_cheb(q,rho) is contained in B_cheb(M^{-1}q, (4/9)rho).
 - Therefore #{M.Walk_k in B(q,rho)} = #{Walk_k in M^{-1}B(q,rho)} <= c_k(M^{-1}q, (4/9)rho). VERIFIED directly: on 232 sampled L7 centres, #(M.L6 in B(q,10)) <= c_6(M^{-1}q, 4.44) with 0 violations.
 - refill(q,rho) := #{Stitch_k in B(q,rho)} <= B (uniform refill bound).
Combine: c_{k+1}(q,rho) <= c_k(M^{-1}q, (4/9)rho) + B.
INDUCTION with invariant c_k(q,r) <= C r + 1 for all q, r in (0,10]:
 c_{k+1}(q,rho) <= C(4/9)rho + 1 + B; this is <= C rho + 1 iff B <= (5/9)C rho, worst at rho=1, i.e. C >= (9/5)B. Base case = the finite base walk, checked. The shrinking radius absorbs the additive B — no kB blow-up — so C is genuinely level-independent.
Verified invariant on real data: max c <= 36 rho + 1 with margins 34/135/335 at rho=1/4/10, both L6 and L7.

ROLE OF THE IRRATIONAL ROTATION (salvaged): equidistribution is NOT a per-ball direction-spreader; it operates ACROSS SCALES. arccos(-1/6) irrational => the renormalization has no resonant closed orbit => the local configuration law converges to a level-INDEPENDENT fixed point (the L4' convergence, measured N81 -> 109.3). That is why the recursion's constant B and hence C are level-uniform. So Route 2 is real but subsumed: it explains level-independence of the recursion, it does not itself count.
routes.key_sublemma: For the route that CLOSES (metric-contraction recursion): UNIFORM REFILL BOUND — there is a level-independent B with #{Stitch_k in any Cheb-rho ball} <= B for all k, q, rho in [1,10] (measured L6->L7: max = 17 at rho=10, <= 3rho curve-like; already certified elsewhere as B<=20, structural cap 44). For Route 2 AS LITERALLY POSED, the sublemma would be "arc tangent directions through a ball equidistribute / are non-concentrated" — this is FALSE (falsified below).
routes.C_derived: Route 2 (equidistribution) alone: NO usable C (premise falsified). Via the count-recursion it enables: C = (9/5)B. Safe/absolute refill cap B=20 -> C = 36 (matches the skeleton's 'naive fixed point 36'); measured curve-like refill (refill_max(rho) <= 3rho, worst ratio 3.0 at rho=1) -> C = (9/5)*3.0 = 5.4, i.e. C=6 suffices — consistent with the empirical max c/rho ~ 2.7. Conservative provable value: C=36 (or C=80 using the proven refill cap 44).
routes.computational_evidence: On real L5/L6/L7 walks: (1) chains rebuild as exact paths (0 bad steps, all distinct). (2) Densest balls have only 1-3 arcs even at rho=10 — re-entry is O(1), not many. (3) Arc directions in densest balls reach |cos|=0.99-0.999 (near-parallel) — equidistribution FALSE per-ball. (4) Motion is ballistic: <|disp|> ~ 1.77 n (n up to 128), MSD/n grows 8.76->405 -> not diffusive; step-direction persistence cos = 0.65/0.50/0.39/0.32 at lag 1/5/20/50. (5) Recursion identity exact: 27,697/27,697 M.L6 points in L7; refill = 65,035; refill_max = 9 (rho4), 17 (rho10). (6) Pullback inequality #(M.L6 in B(q,10)) <= c_6(M^-1 q,4.44): 0 violations / 232 centres. (7) Invariant c <= 36rho+1 holds with margin 34/135/335 at both L6,L7. So the target inequality holds and is delivered by the recursion; the equidistribution mechanism is empirically the wrong picture locally (walk is coherent/ballistic, which is why incidence is linear not quadratic).
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route2_equidist.py (chain rebuild, densest-ball arc decomposition, arc-direction spread) and /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route2_recursion_test.py (exact M.L6-in-L7 identity, refill bound, pullback inequality, invariant c<=36rho+1). Run with PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 pypy3 <script>. Ballistic/persistence measurement in the inline pypy3 command logged in this session.
routes.where_stuck: Route 2 AS AN INDEPENDENT PROOF IS STUCK/DEAD: its mechanism (local tangent directions equidistribute -> no aligned re-entry -> non-dense) is falsified on real data — the walk is locally ballistic and coherent (near-parallel re-entries with |cos| up to 0.999, persistent step directions), which is the OPPOSITE of local equidistribution and is itself the reason incidence is linear. Equidistribution of arccos(-1/6) is genuine but only ACROSS scales (it guarantees the renormalization fixed point / level-independence), not a per-ball counting bound. The TARGET INEQUALITY nevertheless CLOSES via the sibling metric-contraction recursion (identity + ||M^-1||_inf=4/9 + uniform refill B), which I verified exactly on real data, giving C=36 (safe) or C~6 (refined refill). The one remaining unproven link for that closure is the UNIFORM REFILL BOUND B (level-independent cap on stitch points in a Cheb-10 ball) — already certified-finite (<=20; cap 44) in prior work, so the whole lemma reduces to promoting that single measured cap to an all-levels theorem, not to any equidistribution claim.

routes.proof_sketch:
GOAL: level-uniform C with c_k(q,rho) = #{walk pts within Cheb rho of q} <= C*rho + 1 for rho in [1,10].

STEP 1 — the pure separation/packing sub-route is DEAD (proven on data). The minimum inter-point Chebyshev separation is the trivial 1: distinct lattice points, and non-consecutive walk points genuinely sit at Cheb 1 (88 such pairs at L7; min-Cheb by path-gap = 1 even at gap 4-6 at L7). So no metric clearance gap exists. A separation floor s=1 gives only the packing bound (2rho+1)^3 — cubic and useless. Route 3 CANNOT be a metric-packing argument. This is a hard negative result, verified.

STEP 2 — the working reduction is via the PATH structure, not the metric. The walk is a path (each interior point has exactly 2 walk-neighbours) with steps of Cheb-norm <=2. For any ball B(q,rho), partition its walk points by the path index into maximal contiguous ARCS (runs of consecutive indices). Then EXACTLY
    c(q,rho) = sum over arcs of (arc length)  <=  A(rho) * L(rho),
where A(rho) = number of arcs meeting the ball ("return count": how many separate times the path visits the ball) and L(rho) = max arc length ("sojourn": longest single uninterrupted pass staying inside the ball). This is an identity, not an inequality, for the first equality.

STEP 3 — both factors are measured LEVEL-UNIFORM and one is linear, on the real L5/L6/L7 walks (max over ALL centres q):
  A(rho) <= 5 for every rho in [1,10], flat across levels (2 at rho=1 rising to 5 at rho=8-10).
  L(rho) <= ~2.5*rho (slope a = max L/rho = 3.0), level-stable (rho=10 gives 24/25/24 at L5/6/7).
  => c(q,rho) <= A0 * L(rho) is LINEAR and level-uniform: C = A0 * a.

STEP 4 — the constant. Crude factored A0*a = 5*3 = 15. Honest per-centre product max((A*L)-1)/rho = 6.4, so c <= 6.4*rho + 1 holds on all measured data. Tight direct max(c-1)/rho = 3.0. The densest balls confirm the "curve-like" picture: the L6 rho=10 densest ball (c=26) is exactly TWO path-arcs of lengths 14 and 12 — two short near-linear passes, not a packed blob; the rho=6 densest (c=15) is a SINGLE arc.

STEP 5 — the rejected alternative (recorded so it is not retried): the single-quantity recurrence-window bound c(q,rho) <= W(2rho)+1, where W(d)=max path-gap over Cheb-<=d pairs, is BOTH loose (W(20) ~ 200-900) AND non-level-uniform (W(2rho) at rho=8 is 869/875/885 at L5 but 197/213 at L7). It fails because two DIFFERENT arcs through one ball are path-far, so the min..max index window overcounts massively. Only the per-arc decomposition works.

CONSEQUENCE FOR L4': the factored bound gives uniform boundedness with a loose constant (feeds the qualitative "<=36 fixed point" closure that PROOF-SKELETON says suffices for count-contraction), but it does NOT reach the TIGHT load-bearing c(q,4.44) <= 12 — the factored value is 5*L(4.44) ~ 55. The tight 12 comes only from the direct measurement / the anti-stacking (A and L do not co-maximize). So Route 3 yields the qualitative uniform-linear bound, not the tight constant.
routes.key_sublemma: Level-uniform two-factor bound: (RETURN) the number of maximal contiguous path-arcs meeting any Cheb-rho ball is <= A0 = 5, and (SOJOURN) any single contiguous path-pass staying inside a Cheb-rho ball has <= a*rho + b points (a<=3), both uniformly in the level k. The SOJOURN factor is the essential 1-D-ness and is the hard one: triple-freeness alone does not bound it (free triple-free walks reach >=224; box-confined ones can be super-linear), so it relies on the orbit's self-similar structure.
routes.C_derived: Level-uniform LINEAR bound holds with C = 6.4 (honest per-centre A*L product: c(q,rho) <= 6.4*rho + 1 on all L5/L6/L7 data); crude factored A0*a = 15; tight direct C = 3.0 (>= 2.5 asymptotic). The factored bound does NOT deliver the tight load-bearing c(q,4.44) <= 12 (gives ~55); only the direct/anti-stacking route does. So Route 3 => uniform boundedness (~C<=15, enough for the qualitative <=36 count-contraction closure), not the tight ~27/C~2.5.
routes.computational_evidence: Ran on the actual seed-193 orbit walks L5 (8214 pts), L6 (27697), L7 (92732), built from gate2-l7-construction-L{5,6,7}.pkl via word_interiors. (1) Min inter-point Cheb separation = 1 at all levels; non-adjacent Cheb-1 pairs exist (8/23/88) => packing route dead. (2) Max return count A(rho) <= 5 for all rho<=10, level-stable. (3) Max sojourn L(rho) linear, <=2.5rho, level-stable (24/25/24 at rho=10). (4) Direct max_c level-stable: 25/26/27 at rho=10, load-bearing max c(q,4)=10/10/11, c(q,5)=12/12/14. (5) Densest rho=10 ball = 2 arcs (14,12); rho=6 = 1 arc (15) — confirmed curve-like. (6) Recurrence-window W(2rho) blows up and is non-uniform (869 at L5 rho=8) => that variant rejected. (7) Naive sojourn renormalization multiplier = wordlen_max * ||M^-1||inf = 5*(4/9) = 2.222 > 1 => the recursion for the sub-lemma is EXPANSIVE (does not self-close in worst case).
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3_arcs.py (main: per-level arc decomposition A(rho), L(rho), max_c, recurrence window W(d), across L5/L6/L7). Supporting: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route3_separation.py (S0 separation floor, densest-ball arc anatomy, path-gap vs space-gap). Outputs: design/lemma/route3-arcs-all.json, route3-sep-L6.json, route3-CONCLUSION.json.
routes.where_stuck: Route 3 delivers a CLEAN, level-uniform, LINEAR bound c(q,rho) <= A(rho)*L(rho) contingent on ONE sub-lemma: level-uniformity of (return count A<=5) and (sojourn L<=a*rho+b). It also proves a hard negative — the metric separation floor is exactly 1, so any packing/clearance argument is dead; the linear structure MUST come from the path-arc decomposition, not from separation. The remaining gap: PROVING the sojourn/return sub-lemma is not easier than the original incidence bound. Triple-freeness alone does not bound the sojourn linearly, and the naive renormalization recursion for it is expansive (multiplier 5*(4/9)=2.22>1, or 1.51 even with the typical word length 3.4), so it does not self-close in the worst case — it is the SAME hard renormalization kernel L4' already rests on. Route 3 recasts the incidence inequality into a more geometric, more plausible-looking form (bounded returns + linear sojourn, both flat across 3 levels) and kills the packing sub-route, but does not independently discharge the kernel. Additionally, even granting the sub-lemma, the FACTORED constant is loose (C~6.4-15), so Route 3 gives only the qualitative uniform-boundedness closure (<=36 fixed point), not the TIGHT load-bearing c(q,4.44)<=12, which still needs the separate anti-stacking (A,L anti-co-maximization) argument.

routes.route: ROUTE 4 — Direct induction of the incidence bound through the exact recursion Walk_{k+1} = M·Walk_k ⊔ Stitch_k.
routes.proof_sketch:
GOAL: a level-independent C with c_k(q,ρ) := #{walk pts within Chebyshev ρ of q} ≤ Cρ+1 for all k, all centres, ρ∈[1,10].

I prove the STRONGER real-centre statement Q(k): for ALL q∈R^3 and ρ∈[0.5,10], c_k(q,ρ) ≤ Cρ+1 (lattice centres are a special case), plus the trivial fact that ρ<0.5 ⇒ c_k(q,ρ)≤1 (a cube of side 2ρ<1 holds ≤1 integer per axis). This range is closed under the pull-back below.

EXACT INGREDIENTS (all machine-verified on the real L5/L6/L7 chains, none hand-waved):
(1) Recursion identity. anchors = M·(parent chain), interiors = Stitch. Verified EXACTLY that M^-1·anchors(L6)==chain(L5) and M^-1·anchors(L7)==chain(L6) over rationals (Fraction), and that Walk = anchors ⊔ interiors is a disjoint union (all points unique: 8214/27697/92732).
(2) Contraction. M^-1 = [[1/3,0,0],[0,-1/9,1/3],[0,-1/3,0]]; ||M^-1||_∞ = 4/9 (row sums 1/3,4/9,1/3), confirmed exactly and by a 2e5-sample worst-case ratio 0.44444. Hence if Ma∈B_∞(q,ρ) then a−M^-1q = M^-1(Ma−q) has ||·||_∞ ≤ (4/9)ρ, so the dilated-old points in B_∞(q,ρ) inject into B_∞(M^-1q,(4/9)ρ). Therefore #dilated-old ≤ c_k(M^-1q,(4/9)ρ).

INDUCTION STEP. c_{k+1}(q,ρ) = #dilated-old + #stitch ≤ c_k(M^-1q,(4/9)ρ) + B_local(ρ), where B_local(ρ):=sup_{q∈R^3} #{stitch pts in B_∞(q,ρ)}. Pull-back radius (4/9)ρ∈[0.22,4.44].
• ρ∈[1.125,10] ⇒ (4/9)ρ≥0.5: use Q(k): ≤ C(4/9)ρ+1+B_local(ρ). This is ≤ Cρ+1 iff B_local(ρ) ≤ (5/9)Cρ, i.e. C ≥ (9/5)·B_local(ρ)/ρ.
• ρ∈[0.5,1.125) ⇒ (4/9)ρ<0.5: dilated ≤1, so c_{k+1} ≤ 1+B_local(ρ); ≤ Cρ+1 iff C ≥ B_local(ρ)/ρ.
Taking the sup over ρ gives the exact closing condition C* = max( sup_{[0.5,1.125)} B_local/ρ , (9/5)·sup_{[1.125,10]} B_local/ρ ).

MEASURED REFILL (exact, two independent algorithms — a sliding-min-corner cube max and an integer-corner scatter — agree to the unit; Monte-Carlo confirms as a lower bound): B_local(ρ) is linear and level-stable across the L5→L6 and L6→L7 transitions. Worst ratios: B_local(0.5)=3 (ratio 6), B_local(1.5)=5 (ratio 3.33 ⇒ (9/5)·=6.0), decaying to ~2.1 by ρ=10. Both regimes bind at 6.0 ⇒ C* = 6.

BASE CASE. Full-walk real-centre incidence at L5/L6/L7 satisfies c(0.5)≤3, c(1)≤3, …, c(10)≤27, all ≤ 6ρ+1 with wide margin; low levels 0–4 are short walks, trivially bounded by C=6. So the induction closes: c_k(q,ρ) ≤ 6ρ+1 uniformly in k.

HONEST CAVEAT ON THE CONSTANT: C=6 is ~2.4× the TRUE/measured constant (max incidence 27 at ρ=10 ⇒ C_true≈2.7; load-bearing c_k(4.44)=11 measured). Route 4 therefore proves EXISTENCE of a uniform linear bound but does NOT reproduce the tight number: it gives c_k(4.44) ≤ 6·4.44+1 = 27.6, not the ≤12 that L4' wants. The loss is intrinsic to this route: a single connector word deposits up to 5 interior points into a radius-1.5 ball (the ρ=1.5 burst), and the 4/9 contraction cannot dilute a fresh-word burst at small scale; the induction fixed point is (9/5)×refill-density, and while the asymptotic density (2.1) would give ~3.8, the small-ρ word-burst inflates the sup to 6.
routes.key_sublemma: UNIFORM LINEAR REFILL: B_local(ρ) := sup_{q∈R^3} #{Stitch_k points within Chebyshev ρ of q} satisfies B_local(ρ) ≤ 3.33·ρ (more precisely ≤ B_local(0.5)=3 and the tabulated envelope) at EVERY level k, with a level-independent bound. Verified EXACTLY at the two available transitions (k=5,6); its uniformity in k rests on the construction being level-homogeneous (same connector-word domains, bounded word length ≤~7 ⇒ ≤6 interiors per segment, same fragile-first stitch rule), so the stitch's local density does not drift with k.
routes.C_derived: C = 6 (exact closing constant of the induction, given the measured refill). Closing condition: C* = max( sup_{ρ∈[0.5,1.125)} B_local(ρ)/ρ , (9/5)·sup_{ρ∈[1.125,10]} B_local(ρ)/ρ ) = max(6.0, 6.0) = 6.0. This is a valid uniform bound but ~2.4× the true constant (~2.7); it does NOT certify the tight load-bearing figure c_k(4.44)≤12 (it yields ≤27.6).
routes.computational_evidence: Sub-lemma HOLDS on real data and is level-stable. Recursion identity M^-1·anchors==parent-chain verified exactly at L6 and L7. ||M^-1||_∞=4/9 exact. Full incidence over lattice centres reproduces the reported curve-like scaling: max at ρ=10 is 25/26/27 for L5/L6/L7, and c_k(4.44)=10/10/11 ≤ 12 (load-bearing case holds in the DATA). Refill B_local(ρ) (max over L6,L7, exact, corroborated by a 2nd exact method): ρ=0.5→3, 1→3, 1.5→5, 2→6, 3→8, 4→9, 4.44→9, 5→11, 6→13, 7→15, 8→18, 9→20, 10→21 — linear, ratio decays 6→3.33→…→2.1, stable between the two transitions (e.g. B(1.5)=4 vs 5, B(10)=21 vs 20). Base case c_full(ρ)≤6ρ+1 verified with large slack at L5/L6/L7. Every quantitative claim is from a script that ran (route4_incidence.py + two independent verifiers).
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route4_incidence.py (main: builds chains from gate2-l7-construction-L{5,6,7}.pkl, verifies the exact recursion identity M^-1·anchors==parent chain, computes ||M^-1||_∞=4/9 over Fraction, the full lattice-centred incidence table, and the EXACT real-centre refill B_local via a sliding min-corner cube-max; derives C*). /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route4_exactcheck.py (independent EXACT refill via integer-corner scatter — agrees to the unit — plus base-case certification c_full≤6ρ+1). /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route4_verify.py (Monte-Carlo cross-check, a lower bound). Results: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/route4-incidence-results.json. Run with /Users/erik/homelab/tools/pypy3.11-v7.3.20-macos_x86_64/bin/pypy3 (whole run ~42s, ~single-core; light).
routes.where_stuck: TWO honest gaps. (1) EXISTENCE is closed: given the refill sub-lemma, the exact induction proves c_k(q,ρ) ≤ 6ρ+1 uniformly in k. The sub-lemma (B_local(ρ) linear, level-independent) is verified exactly at only the two transitions the data permits (k=5,6); promoting it to ALL k is a structural claim (level-homogeneous construction, bounded word length) that is not yet a theorem — the missing piece is a level-independent proof that the connector-word local density does not drift, ideally bounding B_local without reference to C. (2) The CONSTANT is loose: Route 4 yields C=6, ~2.4× the true/measured ~2.7, because a single connector word dumps up to 5 interior points into a radius-1.5 ball and the 4/9 contraction cannot dilute that fresh-word burst at small scale (the (9/5)×density fixed point is pinned by the ρ=1.5 burst, ratio 3.33). Consequently Route 4 does NOT reproduce the tight load-bearing bound c_k(4.44)≤12 (it gives ≤27.6). Reaching the tight constant needs a different/finer argument (e.g. bounding the refill through the anchor-incidence with per-segment contributions counted after one further dilation, or a direct geometric path argument), not this whole-word-additive induction.

judged.best_route: Route 4 — direct induction of c_k(q,rho)<=C*rho+1 through the EXACT recursion Walk_{k+1}=M·Walk_k ⊔ Stitch_k. Routes 4 and 2 are the SAME mechanism (metric-contraction count-recursion c_{k+1}(q,rho) <= c_k(M^-1 q,(4/9)rho) + B_local(rho)); Route 4 is the strictly more complete write-up. It proves the stronger real-centre statement Q(k) over rho in [0.5,10], correctly closes the induction range under the (4/9) pull-back (including the sub-0.5 collapse c<=1), verifies the recursion identity M^-1·anchors==parent-chain EXACTLY over Fraction at both available transitions, computes ||M^-1||_inf=4/9 exactly, and gives an explicit closing condition C* = max(sup_{[0.5,1.125)}B_local/rho, (9/5)·sup_{[1.125,10]}B_local/rho) = 6. Route 2 rediscovered the identical recursion only after its nominal mechanism (equidistribution) was falsified; Route 3 recasts into arc-return×sojourn but its sub-lemma is admittedly 'not easier than the original' and its renormalization is expansive (2.22>1); Route 1 is dead (kappa_3<=12 is vacuous below Chebyshev length ~515, gives only O(rho^3)).
judged.remaining_gap: ONE gap: the UNIFORM (level-independent) LINEAR REFILL BOUND. Define B_local(rho) := sup_{q in R^3} #{Stitch_k points within Chebyshev rho of q}. The induction closes to c_k <= C*rho+1 for ALL k iff B_local(rho) <= (5/9)C*rho for all k (the shrinking (4/9)-radius absorbs the additive refill, so there is no k·B blow-up). B_local is currently verified EXACTLY only at the two transitions the data permits (k=5,6: e.g. B_local(1.5)=3, B_local(10)=17-19, ratio decaying 6->3.3->2.1, and it is stable between the two levels). Promoting this measured cap to a theorem for every k is the sole missing link. Note it CANNOT be discharged for free by the same induction: a stitch word deposits interiors within ~Cheb-14 of its anchors, so bounding refill by nearby anchors pulls in radius (4/9)(rho+14)>10 with a wordlen coefficient ~7 — non-contractive — hence the refill genuinely needs its own uniform argument. SECOND, weaker caveat (not the target's existence claim but L4's load-bearing need): Route 4's provable constant is C=6, giving c_k(4.44)<=27.6, whereas the load-bearing requirement c_k(4.44)<=(4/9)·27~=12 needs the TIGHT C~2.5. The tight constant is NOT delivered by any route rigorously (only measured: exact lattice max 12 at rho=4 on L7); it needs a separate anti-stacking argument (return-count A and sojourn L do not co-maximize, per Route 3) or per-segment refined refill counting.
judged.combination: YES — the strongest path is an explicit combination, and it is natural because the routes are complementary rather than independent. (1) Route 4/2 EXACT contraction-recursion is the spine: it reduces the whole target to the single refill sub-lemma with a clean explicit closing constant. (2) Route 3 feeds the refill: the Stitch set is itself a union of short bounded-length connector-word arcs, so Route 3's arc machinery (bounded RETURN count A<=5 and linear SOJOURN L<=~2.5rho, both measured level-stable) is exactly the tool to bound B_local(rho) linearly and uniformly — the return/sojourn decomposition applied to the stitch discharges Route 4's gap. Route 3's hard NEGATIVE result (min Chebyshev separation is exactly 1, so any packing/clearance bound is dead and cubic) is also load-bearing guidance: it rules out the packing shortcut, forcing the path-arc route. (3) Route 2's SALVAGED insight supplies the missing conceptual justification for level-independence: the irrational rotation arccos(-1/6) makes the renormalization non-resonant, so the local configuration law converges to a level-INDEPENDENT fixed point (measured N81->109.3) — this is precisely the structural reason B_local does not drift with k, which Route 4 asserts but does not prove. (4) Route 1 contributes only a null result (kappa_3 is vacuous locally) — useful as a proven dead-end, not as an ingredient. So: Route 4 spine + Route 3 arc-bound-on-stitch (existence & the anti-stacking needed for the tight constant) + Route 2 renormalization-fixed-point (level-uniformity) = a coherent combined proof.
judged.next_computation: Attack the uniform refill sub-lemma in two concrete steps. (A) EMPIRICAL drift test: build the L8 chain (budgeted; the note warns an L8 ledger uses ~4 cores, so cap it) and recompute B_local(rho) at the k=6->7 and k=7->8 transitions; if the tabulated envelope (B_local(1.5)=3, B_local(10)~17-21, ratios 3.3->2.1) stays flat across THREE transitions, the level-homogeneity hypothesis is strongly supported and one more level pins whether C can be tightened below 6. (B) STRUCTURAL reduction to a finite check: enumerate the fixed finite menu of connector words (word length <=~7 => <=6 interiors each) and prove that only O(1) word-interiors can enter any Cheb-rho ball uniformly in k — do this by bounding the number of anchors (dilated parent points) within Cheb (rho+wordspan) of q via the induction hypothesis at the pulled-back radius, then multiplying by max interiors/word. This converts B_local's uniformity into (i) a finite enumeration over the word menu (computational) plus (ii) a level-independent bound on local anchor multiplicity (the genuine-analysis residue, tied to Route 2's non-resonance). Separately, to chase the TIGHT constant c(4.44)<=12, run Route 3's anti-stacking measurement: verify max over q of (A(rho)-weighted sojourn product) never reaches A0*L(rho), i.e. that returns and long sojourns are mutually exclusive in the densest balls (data already shows the rho=10 densest ball is 2 arcs of 14+12, not 5×24).

stress.holes_found: Two holes, both already flagged by the judge and now quantitatively confirmed. (1) UNIFORM B_local is measured, not proven — this is the genuine single gap. I stress-tested it at a THIRD transition the write-up lacked (L8 stitch, 219,006 pts, exact corner-max): C* stayed exactly 6.0 across L6/L7/L8, and the binding cells (rho=0.5 -> Csmall=6; rho=1.5 -> Cbig=6) are flat. BUT there is measurable upward drift in non-binding cells: B_local(rho=1.0) went 3->3->4 for the stitch AND 3->3->4 for the full walk across L6/L7/L8. So B_local is NOT perfectly level-invariant; uniformity is empirical (3 transitions, no counterexample) not a theorem, and the induction assumes it as an unproven hypothesis. (2) The TIGHT constant is not delivered: C=6 gives c_k(4.44) <= 6*4.44+1 = 27.6, but L4's load-bearing requirement is c_k(4.44) <= 12 (needs C~2.5). Route 4 proves EXISTENCE of a uniform C, not the tight value L4 consumes; the anti-stacking argument for the tight constant is absent from Route 4. No counterexample walk or diverging level was found.
stress.corrected_status: CLOSES-MODULO-SUBLEMMA (verified, not smuggled). The induction mechanism is rigorous and I independently re-derived its core inequality c_{k+1}(q,rho) <= c_k(M^-1 q,(4/9)rho) + B_local(rho) from ||M^-1||_inf=4/9 (exact) and Cheb=inf-norm — it does NOT assume its conclusion. Recursion identity exact, closing case-split correct, base case holds real-centre (B(10)=30/27<=61). The target existence-inequality (uniform C exists) is genuinely REDUCED to one sub-lemma: level-uniform B_local. That sub-lemma is measured-stable (C*=6 across THREE transitions L6/L7/L8, extending the write-up's two) with no counterexample, but remains UNPROVEN and shows slight upward drift (B(1.0):3->3->4). Separately, the constant Route 4 delivers (C=6) does NOT meet L4's tight load-bearing need (C~2.5); that requires a separate anti-stacking argument outside Route 4.
stress.verdict: The 'proof' is an honest, valid REDUCTION, not a smuggle — the metric-contraction count-recursion is real math and I verified every algebraic step (Minv=4/9, Cheb inequality, disjoint-union identity, case-split). It survives adversarial testing: I ran a third transition (L8) the write-up did not have and C*=6 held. What it is NOT is a completed proof: (a) level-uniformity of B_local is measured across 3 levels with minor drift, not proven — this is the single real gap, exactly as advertised; (b) the constant proven (C=6, so c_k(4.44)<=27.6) falls short of L4's tight need (<=12, C~2.5), which no route delivers rigorously. Route 4 is correctly ranked #1: it cleanly isolates the whole problem down to the uniform-refill sub-lemma with an explicit, correct closing constant, and nothing in it is circular.
